skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guides AI agents through creating, validating, and packaging reusable skills with clear instructions and supporting resources.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package reusable AI skills with structured guidance.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography, hardcoded URL, reconnaissance, and backtick alerts are false positives from license text, prose, and Markdown examples. The confirmed concerns are local filesystem writes and recursive packaging behavior in helper scripts, which are legitimate for this skill but require careful path handling and user review.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Skill Name Can Control Output Path
medium_findings.description: The initializer builds a target path from a user-provided base path and skill name, then writes files into that directory. The script documents naming rules, but it does not enforce them before creating directories and files, so a crafted skill name could direct writes outside the intended output location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script accepts command-line input and uses it directly in path construction before writing files. Legitimate use is clear, but missing validation creates a real local filesystem safety issue.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script recursively adds every file under the selected skill directory to a zip archive. This is expected behavior for a packager, but it can expose unrelated or sensitive files if the input path points at the wrong directory or if secrets are stored inside the skill folder.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly performs recursive archive creation. The risk depends on user-selected paths and local contents, so the finding is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Apache License URLs Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are standard Apache License reference links. They are not used by executable code and do not create network activity or data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain license text. No code path reads these URLs to make a request.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in Markdown, license text, and template strings rather than cryptographic API calls. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited files found ordinary text and validation messages, not cryptographic logic. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in prose and filenames.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown and Template Text
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are Markdown inline-code examples, fenced command examples, and Python template strings. The Python scripts do not call shell execution APIs such as subprocess, os.system, or eval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are documentation formatting and generated example content. No shell backtick execution exists in the reviewed Python code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Validation Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are benign references to validation flow and command-line usage. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment harvesting, process listing, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation or normal validator control flow. They do not inspect system state beyond checking a user-supplied skill folder.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Path Used for File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer uses command-line values to choose where new skill files are created. Add strict skill-name validation and ensure the resolved destination remains within the requested parent directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction and user input source are visible in the cited lines. The impact is local file creation, not remote code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Archive Creation Without Exclusion Rules
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager archives all files found under the skill directory. Add denylisted names or warnings for secrets, caches, generated archives, and hidden files before distribution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Recursive zip creation is confirmed in code. Whether it exposes sensitive data depends on local directory contents at runtime.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance when creating reusable AI skills for agents. This skill provides a structured workflow for designing, writing, validating, and packaging skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure a skill with SKILL.md and optional resource folders.
Guides users through gathering examples before writing skill instructions.
Provides a helper script that initializes a template skill folder.
Provides a validator for frontmatter and naming checks.
Provides a packager that validates a skill and creates a zip archive.
Describes when to use scripts, references, and assets in a skill.
limitations: The helper scripts perform only basic validation and do not enforce every marketplace rule.
Generated templates still require human editing before publication.
Packaging can include unwanted files if the selected directory is not reviewed first.
The skill is focused on skill creation, not general project scaffolding.

use_cases.title: Create an Internal Agent Skill
use_cases.description: Define a repeatable workflow, choose supporting resources, and initialize a skill folder for team use.

use_cases.title: Validate a Skill Before Sharing
use_cases.description: Check required frontmatter, naming rules, and basic folder structure before handing a skill to others.

use_cases.title: Package a Finished Skill
use_cases.description: Run validation and create a distributable archive after reviewing the folder contents.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a First Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a new AI skill for this workflow: [describe workflow]. Identify the trigger, core steps, and needed resources.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a Skill Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Create the starter structure after the concept is clear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill creator workflow to initialize a skill named [skill-name] in [target path]. Include the resource folders that fit this use case.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Instructions
prompt_templates.scenario: Refine a draft SKILL.md for clarity and trigger quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill folder and improve its SKILL.md. Keep the instructions concise and make the trigger description specific.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Package for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this skill folder, identify any structural issues, review package contents for unwanted files, and prepare it for distribution.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for a monthly reporting workflow.
output_examples.output: A planned skill structure with a clear trigger, a short instruction outline, and recommended reference files for report rules.

output_examples.input: Check whether this skill is ready to package.
Package this completed skill for sharing.
output_examples.output: A validation-first packaging workflow with a reminder to inspect the archive contents before distribution.

best_practices: Start with concrete user examples before writing the skill instructions.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed references into separate files.
Review generated resource files and archive contents before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not publish a template skill that still contains placeholder text.
Do not add resource folders that the skill never uses.
Do not package directories that contain secrets, caches, or unrelated files.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create reusable AI skills with clear triggers, concise instructions, and optional supporting resources.

faq.question: Does it write files automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. The initializer script creates a skill folder and starter files when run with a target path.

faq.question: Can it validate a skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a basic validator for frontmatter, naming, and required SKILL.md structure.

faq.question: Can it package a skill for distribution?
faq.answer: Yes. The package script validates the folder and creates a zip archive from the selected skill directory.

faq.question: What should users review before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should review the selected directory for secrets, unrelated files, hidden files, and leftover placeholders.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Claude Code, and Codex as supported tools.
